cards.question: What property makes a Binary Search Tree (BST) efficient for search?
cards.answer: Every left child is smaller and every right child is larger than the parent, enabling O(log n) average-case search.

cards.question: What is the height of a complete binary tree with n nodes?
cards.answer: ⌊log₂n⌋ — logarithmic in the number of nodes, which is why balanced trees are efficient.

cards.question: Name the three standard tree traversal orders.
cards.answer: Inorder (Left → Root → Right), Preorder (Root → Left → Right), Postorder (Left → Right → Root).

cards.question: What is an AVL tree and why is it used?
cards.answer: A self-balancing BST where the height difference between left and right subtrees is at most 1. Guarantees O(log n) operations in the worst case.
